title: Snoring ENT review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports snoring, witnessed apnoeas, unrefreshing or fragmented sleep, waking headache, sleepiness or fatigue, nocturia, choking, cognitive symptoms, obesity context, Epworth Sleepiness Scale, and STOP-Bang, while prohibiting use of Epworth alone to determine referral.

source.title: Obstructive sleep apnoea/hypopnoea syndrome and obesity hypoventilation syndrome in over 16s
source.population: People aged over 16 years with suspected or diagnosed obstructive sleep apnoea/hypopnoea syndrome.
source.setting: Initial assessment, sleep-service referral, diagnostic testing, treatment discussion, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult OSAHS symptom, assessment, sleep-study, referral, driving-risk, treatment-discussion, and monitoring fields; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE sleep-service or driving pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports snoring, witnessed apnoeas, unrefreshing or fragmented sleep, waking headache, sleepiness or fatigue, nocturia, choking, cognitive symptoms, obesity context, Epworth Sleepiness Scale, and STOP-Bang, while prohibiting use of Epworth alone to determine referral.

assessment.rationale: The adult exact section supports interval snoring history, witnessed breathing disturbance, sleep quality, daytime impact, functional safety and comorbidity context without asserting OSAHS.

source.title: Obstructive sleep apnoea/hypopnoea syndrome and obesity hypoventilation syndrome in over 16s

assessment.text: Supports documenting sleep symptoms and selected comorbidity context in children without diagnosing obstructive sleep apnoea or ordering polysomnography.
assessment.rationale: The paediatric exact section supports caregiver-reported snoring and sleep-related comorbidity context plus ENT examination without recommending tonsillectomy or polysomnography.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Tonsillectomy in Children (Update)
source.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE paediatric ENT, anaesthesia, analgesia, and postoperative adaptation
source.population: Children aged 1 to 18 years being considered for or followed after tonsillectomy.
source.setting: Any clinician setting involved in paediatric tonsillectomy assessment, perioperative education, and postoperative outcome review.
source.exclusions: Exact for recurrent throat-infection documentation, modifying factors, sleep-related comorbidity context, perioperative education, postoperative pain or bleeding review, and outcome assessment. It does not generate surgery, analgesia, antibiotic, steroid, dose, admission, or discharge instructions.

exact_location.heading: Key action statements 4–6 — sleep-disordered breathing and comorbidity context

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting sleep symptoms and selected comorbidity context in children without diagnosing obstructive sleep apnoea or ordering polysomnography.

assessment.text: Supports tailored follow-up, CPAP review, annual review after optimisation, symptoms, Epworth score, vigilance and driving, AHI or ODI, adherence, treatment experience, sleep hygiene, and clinician-reviewed sleep-study follow-up.
assessment.rationale: The exact adult follow-up section supports documenting clinician-entered review plans and follow-up without creating device, testing, or restriction advice.

curation_policy: Direct full-source guideline reconstruction; every legacy item has an auditable comparison and every final clinical item retains exact source provenance.
